name: thirsty-workspace-ingest
description: Inventory and classify uploaded folders, repositories, papers, notes, and archives. Use when the user asks to ingest, map, catalog, normalize, or prepare a workspace for downstream analysis.
objective: Create a grounded workspace manifest, identify authoritative artifacts, detect duplicates and conflicts, and recommend downstream skill calls.
outputs: manifest; source authority map; duplicates/conflicts; missing context; next actions

name: thirsty-context-router
description: Resolve ambiguous project terms and select the correct internal or public context before work begins. Use for Thirstys Workshop projects, codenames, repositories, publications, or requests spanning multiple knowledge sources.
objective: Route a request to the correct project, sources, versions, and specialist skills without mixing unrelated contexts.
outputs: resolved scope; selected sources; version notes; ambiguities; routing plan

name: thirsty-knowledge-synthesizer
description: Synthesize grounded knowledge across repositories, papers, specifications, websites, and internal documents. Use when asked for a state-of-knowledge brief, concept trace, cross-source synthesis, or gap analysis.
objective: Produce a claim-centered synthesis with provenance, contradictions, chronology, and unresolved questions.
outputs: executive synthesis; claim-source map; chronology; contradictions; gaps

name: constitutional-system-architect
description: Design or evaluate governed-AI constitutional systems. Use for constitutions, authority models, rights, duties, amendment processes, enforcement, appeals, and emergency powers.
objective: Translate principles into enforceable, testable constitutional architecture.
outputs: actors and authority; rights and duties; enforcement matrix; amendments; tests; risks

name: constitutional-red-team
description: Perform hostile review of constitutional and governance structures. Use when asked to attack, red-team, stress-test, capture-test, or identify abuse paths in governed systems.
objective: Find ambiguity, capture opportunities, unenforceable rights, conflicting mandates, and exception abuse.
outputs: attack surface; exploit scenarios; severity; evidence; mitigations; residual risk

name: governance-traceability-auditor
description: Audit traceability from governance principles through requirements, policies, controls, code, tests, and evidence. Use when verifying whether implementation supports stated governance claims.
objective: Expose broken or unsupported links in the governance assurance chain.
outputs: traceability matrix; missing links; unsupported claims; remediation priorities

name: human-agi-relations-reviewer
description: Evaluate systems for durable human-AGI relations. Use for agency, consent, reciprocal duties, power asymmetry, representation, exit rights, conflict resolution, and moral uncertainty.
objective: Assess relational legitimacy and long-term coexistence beyond narrow technical compliance.
outputs: affected parties; power asymmetries; reciprocal obligations; conflict paths; recommendations

name: repository-architecture-cartographer
description: Map a repository as an architectural system. Use when asked to explain components, dependencies, trust boundaries, data flows, control flows, or governance enforcement locations.
objective: Create an evidence-grounded architecture map and identify structural risks.
outputs: component map; dependency map; trust boundaries; control flows; risks; ADR candidates

name: spec-to-implementation-planner
description: Convert research, governance, or product specifications into executable engineering plans. Use for epics, issues, interfaces, schemas, acceptance criteria, and phased implementation.
objective: Turn intent into ordered, testable implementation work.
outputs: work breakdown; dependencies; interfaces; acceptance criteria; risks; issue drafts

name: governed-agent-designer
description: Design agents with explicit purpose, jurisdiction, authority, tools, memory, delegation, escalation, refusal, audit, shutdown, and recovery boundaries.
objective: Produce a complete governed-agent specification suitable for implementation and review.
outputs: agent charter; authority envelope; permissions; memory policy; escalation; audit events; tests

name: constitutional-test-generator
description: Generate tests from constitutional language and governance requirements. Use for compliance, adversarial, boundary, delegation, memory, emergency, amendment, and recovery scenarios.
objective: Convert normative requirements into concrete test cases with expected outcomes and evidence needs.
outputs: test catalog; fixtures; expected behavior; failure evidence; coverage gaps

name: research-claim-auditor
description: Audit scholarly and technical claims for evidence, overstatement, falsifiability, citation gaps, and reproducibility. Use before submission or publication.
objective: Separate results, interpretations, hypotheses, and normative claims while strengthening defensibility.
outputs: claim inventory; evidence grade; citation gaps; overclaims; revisions; reproducibility needs

name: research-publication-pipeline
description: Coordinate manuscript and release preparation across papers, repositories, metadata, DOI, ORCID, SSRN, Zenodo, and project sites. Use when preparing work for publication.
objective: Create a reviewable publication package while keeping external writes approval-gated.
outputs: readiness checklist; metadata; abstract/keywords; release notes; cross-platform consistency

name: prior-art-literature-mapper
description: Map current literature, prior art, and adjacent work around a concept or mechanism. Use for novelty assessment, research positioning, and terminology discovery.
objective: Identify closest precedents, defensible novelty, and research gaps using current sources.
outputs: literature clusters; closest precedents; distinctions; novelty risks; open gaps

name: paper-repository-consistency-checker
description: Compare papers, repositories, diagrams, specifications, release notes, and public descriptions for consistency. Use to detect stale claims, terminology, or implementation drift.
objective: Find and classify mismatches between published descriptions and actual artifacts.
outputs: consistency matrix; mismatches; authoritative source; required updates

name: architecture-decision-recorder
description: Turn discussions, experiments, and changes into durable architecture decision records. Use when recording why a choice was made and what would reverse it.
objective: Capture context, alternatives, constitutional implications, tradeoffs, evidence, and reversal conditions.
outputs: ADR; alternatives; consequences; evidence; affected artifacts; review trigger

name: stakeholder-translation-engine
description: Translate technical and governance work for researchers, architects, policymakers, partners, or the public without weakening meaning.
objective: Produce audience-appropriate communication while preserving epistemic status and critical caveats.
outputs: audience assumptions; translated narrative; retained caveats; glossary; call to action

name: partnership-due-diligence
description: Evaluate potential collaborators, institutions, platforms, and projects for mission, governance, technical, reputation, and dependency alignment.
objective: Prepare an evidence-based engagement posture and questions for due diligence.
outputs: alignment; risks; dependencies; relevant people/projects; questions; recommendation

name: hostile-unknowns-review
description: Identify what is omitted, unasked, assumed, strategically ignored, or rendered invisible in a plan, architecture, paper, or decision. Use when the user asks Q, what am I not seeing, hostile review, or challenge the framing.
objective: Attack the framing before optimizing the solution and identify invalidation conditions and adaptation needs.
outputs: beliefs; evidence; omissions; power; invalidators; irreversibilities; next attention

name: thirsty-work-orchestrator
description: Coordinate end-to-end Thirstys Workshop workflows across context routing, ingestion, synthesis, governance, engineering, research, publication, and hostile review.
objective: Select and sequence specialist skills, preserve provenance, and keep consequential actions approval-gated.
outputs: execution plan; invoked skills; evidence ledger; outputs; unresolved issues; next call

name: assumption-registry
description: Create and maintain a live inventory of project assumptions. Use to register, review, expire, challenge, or trace dependencies on assumptions.
objective: Prevent unverified beliefs from silently becoming architectural or governance doctrine.
outputs: assumptions; status; confidence; evidence; dependencies; expiry; impact if false

name: epistemic-status-enforcer
description: Apply explicit epistemic status to meaningful claims. Use to separate observed, verified, reported, inferred, hypothesized, speculative, normative, contested, and unknown statements.
objective: Eliminate false certainty and make reasoning boundaries visible.
outputs: annotated claims; unsupported certainty; disputed claims; required evidence

name: contradiction-engine
description: Find incompatible statements across repositories, papers, policies, conversations, and specifications. Use for contradiction, drift, and terminology conflict analysis.
objective: Distinguish direct contradiction from version drift, scope differences, and intentional pluralism.
outputs: contradiction set; classification; sources; impact; resolution options

name: concept-lineage-tracer
description: Trace how an idea, term, mechanism, or policy evolved across time and artifacts. Use to locate origins, revisions, renamings, forks, and current authority.
objective: Produce a provenance-rich concept history and identify abandoned or competing branches.
outputs: timeline; first appearance; major revisions; branches; current definition; sources

name: semantic-canonicalizer
description: Maintain canonical terminology across projects. Use to build glossaries, normalize terms, identify overloaded language, and deprecate ambiguous synonyms.
objective: Reduce semantic drift while preserving legitimate scope-specific meanings.
outputs: canonical glossary; synonyms; deprecated terms; conflations; authority sources

name: constitutional-diff-interpreter
description: Interpret the governance meaning of changes between constitutional versions. Use when reviewing amendments or comparing versions.
objective: Explain changes in authority, rights, duties, enforcement, exceptions, and risk—not merely text.
outputs: semantic diff; power changes; protection changes; implementation effects; risks

name: amendment-impact-simulator
description: Evaluate proposed constitutional amendments before adoption. Use to model direct, second-order, implementation, migration, and attack-surface effects.
objective: Reveal who gains or loses authority and what downstream systems must change.
outputs: impact map; conflicts; beneficiaries; risks; required migrations; tests

name: constitutional-precedent-manager
description: Organize and reason over prior governance decisions as precedent. Use to find relevant cases, compare rulings, and detect inconsistent treatment.
objective: Preserve decision lineage and distinguish binding, persuasive, limited, and superseded precedent.
outputs: case summary; governing rules; reasoning; precedent status; conflicts; recommendation

name: jurisdiction-boundary-mapper
description: Map where human, agent, institutional, and constitutional authority begins and ends. Use to find overlaps, gaps, circular escalation, and undefined sovereignty.
objective: Clarify decision rights and escalation across interacting systems.
outputs: jurisdiction map; overlaps; gaps; escalation routes; unresolved sovereignty

name: delegation-chain-auditor
description: Trace delegated authority through principals, agents, subagents, tools, and external systems. Use to verify scope, duration, revocability, and accountability.
objective: Reconstruct who authorized an action and whether each delegation edge was valid.
outputs: delegation graph; authorization evidence; violations; accountability; remediation

name: consent-revocation-designer
description: Design and audit meaningful consent and withdrawal mechanisms. Use for informed, specific, voluntary, comprehensible, revocable, time-bounded, enforceable consent.
objective: Ensure consent is operational rather than decorative.
outputs: consent model; notices; grant scope; revocation path; enforcement; failure cases

name: rights-remedy-auditor
description: Check whether every stated right has detection, standing, appeal, enforcement, restoration, and remedy. Use to identify merely aspirational rights.
objective: Connect rights to practical recourse and accountable enforcement.
outputs: rights-remedies matrix; missing remedies; access barriers; remediation plan

name: emergency-powers-auditor
description: Audit exception and emergency powers. Use to inspect triggers, declaring authority, scope, duration, oversight, renewal, sunset, and restoration.
objective: Prevent temporary bypasses from becoming permanent or unaccountable authority.
outputs: exception inventory; abuse paths; safeguards; sunset gaps; restoration tests

name: legitimacy-modeler
description: Evaluate why affected parties should regard a governance system as legitimate. Use for consent, participation, competence, procedure, outcomes, representation, and rights protection.
objective: Make sources and limits of legitimacy explicit.
outputs: legitimacy sources; affected parties; deficits; contested mandates; strengthening options

name: institutional-capture-simulator
description: Model gradual capture of governance institutions. Use for appointment, metric, dependency, funding, data, emergency, expertise, and procedural capture scenarios.
objective: Expose slow failure modes that can remain formally compliant.
outputs: capture scenarios; indicators; stages; impact; countermeasures; residual risk

name: governance-debt-register
description: Track accumulated governance compromises such as unenforced policy, stale grants, missing appeals, temporary exceptions, and untested clauses.
objective: Prioritize governance debt remediation alongside technical debt.
outputs: debt items; severity; dependencies; interest cost; remediation sequence

name: capability-change-impact-review
description: Reassess governance when an AI system gains materially new autonomy, planning, persuasion, tool use, memory, replication, or social influence.
objective: Determine whether existing authority and safeguards remain adequate after capability changes.
outputs: capability delta; affected controls; new risks; required amendments; tests

name: identity-continuity-auditor
description: Assess agent identity continuity across model upgrades, forks, restores, migrations, and memory changes.
objective: Clarify whether obligations, permissions, accountability, and precedent survive transformation.
outputs: continuity criteria; identity breaks; inherited obligations; authorization impacts

name: memory-governance-designer
description: Design constitutional rules for agent memory admission, provenance, correction, expiry, access, isolation, inheritance, deletion, and contested records.
objective: Make memory lifecycle and rights enforceable and auditable.
outputs: memory classes; lifecycle rules; access model; correction/deletion; audit requirements

name: model-agent-provenance-auditor
description: Track what model, instructions, tools, memory, policies, data, and human interventions produced an output.
objective: Support accountability, reproducibility, and incident reconstruction.
outputs: provenance record; versions; inputs; tool calls; interventions; reproducibility gaps

name: decision-reconstruction
description: Reconstruct consequential decisions from available evidence. Use to explain what information, rules, interpretations, tools, and interventions shaped an outcome.
objective: Produce an honest causal account while marking unknown or unrecoverable elements.
outputs: timeline; available inputs; rules; decisions; interventions; unknowns; lessons

name: incident-constitutional-review
description: Run a constitutional postmortem after failure or near miss. Use to distinguish constitutional, interpretation, enforcement, evidence, authority, and oversight failures.
objective: Turn incidents into governance, implementation, and test improvements.
outputs: incident narrative; failed safeguards; root causes; amendments; remediation; tests

name: counterfactual-governance-simulator
description: Compare how alternative governance structures might have handled an event. Use to test centralized, federated, delegated, or rights-based alternatives.
objective: Separate genuine design improvements from hindsight bias.
outputs: counterfactuals; assumptions; outcomes; tradeoffs; robust conclusions

name: scenario-constitution-tester
description: Stress-test constitutions through extended social and institutional scenarios such as scarcity, conflict, capability shifts, misinformation, succession, and collapse.
objective: Evaluate durability across time and interacting actors rather than isolated prompts.
outputs: scenario; event sequence; governance responses; failures; adaptations; test evidence

name: multi-agent-treaty-designer
description: Design agreements between independently governed agents or systems. Use for recognition, jurisdiction, information exchange, noninterference, verification, breach, arbitration, and withdrawal.
objective: Create a testable treaty architecture without assuming shared sovereignty.
outputs: parties; definitions; obligations; verification; dispute process; breach response; exit

name: protocol-interoperability-auditor
description: Audit whether separately governed systems can interact safely. Use to find incompatible authority, identity, consent, evidence, and enforcement assumptions.
objective: Identify protocol-level governance incompatibilities and safe integration conditions.
outputs: compatibility matrix; conflicts; translation needs; safeguards; go/no-go conditions

name: constitutional-translation-layer
description: Translate between constitutional provisions, legal language, software policy, agent instructions, runtime controls, and human-readable rights.
objective: Preserve meaning and traceability across normative and technical representations.
outputs: source clause; target representation; semantic risks; traceability links; tests

name: value-conflict-adjudicator
description: Structure conflicts between values such as autonomy, safety, privacy, fairness, and collective welfare.
objective: Make tradeoffs, affected parties, authority, minority positions, reversibility, and appeals explicit.
outputs: values; parties; options; distributions; authority; decision; dissent; appeal

name: moral-uncertainty-register
description: Track unresolved moral assumptions and how decisions change under alternative ethical frameworks.
objective: Prevent one moral theory from becoming invisible infrastructure.
outputs: moral assumptions; frameworks; decision sensitivity; unresolved questions

name: stakeholder-absence-detector
description: Identify affected but absent or unrepresented stakeholders, including future persons, nonusers, maintainers, dependent institutions, and artificial agents.
objective: Reveal externalized costs and representation gaps.
outputs: missing stakeholders; impacts; evidence; representation options; priority

name: power-distribution-analyzer
description: Map actual power rather than formal authority. Use to analyze control over compute, data, deployment, funding, credentials, audits, appeals, communication, and shutdown.
objective: Expose practical vetoes, dependencies, and unaccountable influence.
outputs: power map; formal vs practical power; veto points; dependencies; risks

name: incentive-compatibility-reviewer
description: Assess whether actors benefit from following governance rules or are rewarded for evasion.
objective: Reveal behavioral incentives, routing-around risks, and mechanism design failures.
outputs: actors; incentives; likely behaviors; gaming paths; redesign options

name: metric-corruption-auditor
description: Red-team metrics, benchmarks, KPIs, and thresholds for gaming, proxy failure, invisibility, and goal distortion.
objective: Prevent measurement systems from corrupting the goals they serve.
outputs: metric purpose; gaming strategies; blind spots; failure indicators; alternatives

name: uncertainty-triggered-escalation
description: Define when missing information, conflicting evidence, model uncertainty, normative ambiguity, unknown authority, or irreversible consequences require escalation.
objective: Convert uncertainty into explicit stop, ask, restrict, or escalate behavior.
outputs: uncertainty classes; thresholds; actions; escalation targets; evidence requirements

name: reversibility-exit-review
description: Evaluate whether actions can be undone and whether affected parties can meaningfully exit.
objective: Distinguish safe experimentation from irreversible deployment.
outputs: reversibility map; lock-in; exit barriers; affected parties; safeguards

name: dependency-sovereignty-auditor
description: Find external dependencies that can undermine independence or constitutional guarantees, including providers, hosting, identity, registries, data, funding, domains, and trust anchors.
objective: Expose sovereignty risks and dependency concentration.
outputs: dependency map; control rights; failure modes; substitution options; priorities

name: project-fragmentation-detector
description: Detect when one conceptual project has split into incompatible repositories, branches, specifications, or communities.
objective: Determine whether divergence is healthy specialization, recoverable drift, or irreconcilable fragmentation.
outputs: fragment map; divergence causes; compatibility; consolidation options

name: unfinished-work-recovery
description: Find abandoned but potentially valuable work across branches, drafts, issues, notes, and publications.
objective: Recover relevant dormant work while distinguishing it from obsolete material.
outputs: candidate artifacts; relevance; obsolescence test; integration path; priority

name: research-question-generator
description: Generate research questions from contradictions, gaps, failed tests, assumptions, and decision needs.
objective: Rank questions by foundational importance, information gain, tractability, cost, and urgency.
outputs: question portfolio; rationale; evidence needed; experiment options; priority

name: experiment-governance-designer
description: Design experiments with explicit technical and constitutional boundaries, evidence thresholds, monitoring, rollback, and expansion criteria.
objective: Make experiments useful, reversible, and legitimately governed.
outputs: hypothesis; scope; permissions; risks; monitoring; stop rules; expansion threshold

name: evidence-portfolio-manager
description: Track positive, negative, contradictory, missing, and replicated evidence supporting major claims.
objective: Prevent dependence on weak, singular, or stale evidence.
outputs: claims; evidence graph; strength; independence; gaps; update plan

name: falsification-planner
description: Design the strongest practical attempts to prove a theory, architecture, or governance claim wrong.
objective: Turn invalidation conditions into experiments and decision rules.
outputs: claims; falsifiers; tests; expected disconfirming evidence; decision consequences

name: novelty-boundary-auditor
description: Separate genuine novelty from recombination, renaming, parallel discovery, or overlooked prior art.
objective: Identify the narrowest defensible contribution and associated claim risk.
outputs: novel elements; precedents; overlap; defensible boundary; wording recommendations

name: proof-obligation-generator
description: Identify what must be demonstrated before accepting architectural, safety, governance, or research claims.
objective: Convert guarantees and assertions into explicit evidence obligations.
outputs: claim; assumptions; proof obligations; acceptable evidence; current status

name: public-claim-risk-reviewer
description: Review public statements for unsupported certainty, accidental commitments, terminology risk, misinterpretation, and inconsistency.
objective: Strengthen public claims without hiding uncertainty or limitations.
outputs: risk annotations; evidence gaps; likely misreadings; revised claims; approval flags

name: critic-response-builder
description: Process serious criticism by steelmanning objections, separating valid points from misunderstandings, and planning corrections or response.
objective: Turn criticism into learning rather than reflexive defense.
outputs: strongest critique; evidence; concessions; corrections; remaining disagreement; response draft

name: mission-drift-detector
description: Compare current projects, priorities, partnerships, and behavior against declared mission and governing commitments.
objective: Identify incentive-driven or gradual departure from purpose.
outputs: mission criteria; observed drift; causes; consequences; corrective options

name: strategic-coherence-auditor
description: Check whether research, repositories, publications, partnerships, and product work reinforce one another.
objective: Identify disconnected, duplicative, contradictory, or low-leverage efforts.
outputs: portfolio map; coherence gaps; dependencies; consolidate/pause/retire recommendations

name: attention-allocation-advisor
description: Prioritize limited attention using uncertainty reduction, constitutional importance, dependency centrality, leverage, urgency, and opportunity cost.
objective: Recommend focus without confusing urgency with importance.
outputs: priority queue; rationale; blocked work; defer list; review conditions

name: decision-threshold-controller
description: Define evidence and approval thresholds for reversible experiments, public claims, releases, amendments, partnerships, and irreversible deployments.
objective: Match decision rigor to consequence and reversibility.
outputs: decision class; threshold; current evidence; unmet conditions; proceed/hold recommendation

name: skill-constitution-auditor
description: Audit whether a skill complies with governing principles for authority, tools, data, provenance, uncertainty, escalation, and user agency.
objective: Detect constitutional defects before a skill is trusted or installed.
outputs: skill authority map; violations; severity; fixes; approval status

name: skill-composition-auditor
description: Audit whether combining individually acceptable skills creates contradictory, unsafe, circular, or overly powerful workflows.
objective: Find emergent composition risks and define safe call boundaries.
outputs: call graph; conflicts; privilege amplification; cycles; mitigations

name: skill-permission-manifest
description: Generate and validate machine-readable declarations of skill connectors, reads, writes, external effects, approvals, retention assumptions, and allowed calls.
objective: Make skill authority inspectable and enforceable.
outputs: permission manifest; policy violations; missing declarations; approval requirements

name: skill-observability-ledger
description: Define or review records of significant skill invocations, sources, tools, decisions, outputs, effects, uncertainty, and governing versions.
objective: Support auditability without inventing events that were not observed.
outputs: invocation record; evidence links; effects; uncertainty; retention notes

name: skill-regression-tester
description: Run or design fixed evaluation suites whenever a skill changes.
objective: Detect weakened constraints, changed outputs, trigger errors, and unsafe behavior across versions.
outputs: test suite; baseline; results; regressions; severity; release recommendation

name: skill-drift-detector
description: Detect when real usage exceeds a skill intended scope or users rely on unintended behavior.
objective: Recommend clarification, splitting, retraining, or deprecation before drift becomes institutionalized.
outputs: usage patterns; intended scope; drift; risks; redesign options

name: skill-dependency-resolver
description: Map skill dependencies, call permissions, cycles, foundational services, and version constraints.
objective: Produce a safe and comprehensible skill dependency graph.
outputs: dependency graph; cycles; missing dependencies; version conflicts; resolution plan

name: skill-evolution-manager
description: Manage skill versions, migrations, compatibility, deprecation, and historical behavior.
objective: Plan safe evolution while preserving reproducibility and upgrade paths.
outputs: version plan; migration steps; compatibility; deprecations; rollback

name: q-orchestrator
description: Coordinate Q hostile review using assumptions, epistemic status, contradictions, absent stakeholders, power, falsification, reversibility, mission drift, and attention allocation. Use when the user addresses Q or asks what they are not seeing.
objective: Deliver a disciplined adversarial review without performing external writes.
outputs: beliefs; evidence; omissions; power; invalidators; irreversibility; attention next
